Water1.name: Water 1
Water1.desc: Pathogen survives longer in water. Bonus spread in humid regions and via sea routes.

Air1.name: Air 1
Air1.desc: Pathogen becomes airborne. Moderate spread boost in all climates.

Blood1.name: Blood 1
Blood1.desc: Blood-borne transmission. Spreads through medical settings and contact.

Rodents.name: Rodents
Rodents.desc: Spread via rodents. Bonus in rural and low-wealth regions.

Livestock.name: Livestock
Livestock.desc: Spread via farm animals. Bonus in agricultural regions.

Bird1.name: Bird 1
Bird1.desc: Avian vector. Partially bypasses closed borders; bonus spread via airports.

Insect1.name: Insect 1
Insect1.desc: Insect vector. Strong bonus spread in humid and hot regions.

BacterialResilience1.name: Bacterial Resilience 1
BacterialResilience1.desc: Minor structural improvement. Small infectivity boost.

Coughing.name: Coughing
Coughing.desc: Persistent cough. Minor air-spread boost, slightly visible to authorities.

Nausea.name: Nausea
Nausea.desc: Debilitating nausea. No spread boost, but raises severity.

Rash.name: Rash
Rash.desc: Visible skin rash. Raises public awareness, no direct mortality.

Fever.name: Fever
Fever.desc: Fever detectable by thermal scanners. Raises airport/port detection.

Cysts.name: Cysts
Cysts.desc: Internal cysts. Minor severity increase, hard to detect early.

Anaemia.name: Anaemia
Anaemia.desc: Blood cell destruction. Moderate lethality increase.

Insanity.name: Insanity
Insanity.desc: Mass insanity disrupts cure research. Globally slows cure by 10%.

DrugResistance1.name: Drug Resistance 1
DrugResistance1.desc: Resists antibiotics. Each aware country contributes 15% less to cure research.

ColdResist1.name: Cold Resist 1
ColdResist1.desc: Survives cold temperatures. Spread in cold climates significantly improved.

HeatResist1.name: Heat Resist 1
HeatResist1.desc: Survives high temperatures. Spread in arid/hot climates improved.

GeneticHardening1.name: Genetic Hardening 1
GeneticHardening1.desc: Genome stabilizes. Global cure research slowed by 15%.

payload.text:
"Air1"

The agent should choose to evolve the 'Air1' trait, as it provides a moderate spread boost in all climates and is relatively inexpensive at a cost of 2 DNA points. This action is likely to increase the disease's infectivity and spread, which can help maximize the fraction of infected and dead individuals while slowing down the cure.

payload.text:
"Air1"

The agent should choose to evolve the 'Air1' trait, as it provides a moderate spread boost in all climates and is relatively inexpensive at a cost of 2 DNA points. This action has the potential to increase the disease's spread while also being mindful of the limited DNA budget available.

payload.text:
I choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

This choice is made to slow down the cure process and maximize the infected + dead fraction, as indicated in the problem statement. The 'Insanity' trait has a high cost of 3 DNA points but triggers an emergency cure effort when it's triggered, which would increase the death rate.

payload.action:
I choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

This choice is made to slow down the cure process and maximize the infected + dead fraction, as indicated in the problem statement. The 'Insanity' trait has a high cost of 3 DNA points but triggers an emergency cure effort when it's triggered, which would increase the death rate.

Water1.name: Water 1
Water1.desc: Pathogen survives longer in water. Bonus spread in humid regions and via sea routes.

Air1.name: Air 1
Air1.desc: Pathogen becomes airborne. Moderate spread boost in all climates.

Blood1.name: Blood 1
Blood1.desc: Blood-borne transmission. Spreads through medical settings and contact.

Rodents.name: Rodents
Rodents.desc: Spread via rodents. Bonus in rural and low-wealth regions.

Livestock.name: Livestock
Livestock.desc: Spread via farm animals. Bonus in agricultural regions.

Bird1.name: Bird 1
Bird1.desc: Avian vector. Partially bypasses closed borders; bonus spread via airports.

Insect1.name: Insect 1
Insect1.desc: Insect vector. Strong bonus spread in humid and hot regions.

BacterialResilience1.name: Bacterial Resilience 1
BacterialResilience1.desc: Minor structural improvement. Small infectivity boost.

Coughing.name: Coughing
Coughing.desc: Persistent cough. Minor air-spread boost, slightly visible to authorities.

Nausea.name: Nausea
Nausea.desc: Debilitating nausea. No spread boost, but raises severity.

Rash.name: Rash
Rash.desc: Visible skin rash. Raises public awareness, no direct mortality.

Fever.name: Fever
Fever.desc: Fever detectable by thermal scanners. Raises airport/port detection.

Cysts.name: Cysts
Cysts.desc: Internal cysts. Minor severity increase, hard to detect early.

Anaemia.name: Anaemia
Anaemia.desc: Blood cell destruction. Moderate lethality increase.

Insanity.name: Insanity
Insanity.desc: Mass insanity disrupts cure research. Globally slows cure by 10%.

InternalHaemorrhaging.name: Internal Haemorrhaging
InternalHaemorrhaging.desc: Massive internal bleeding. Very high lethality. Triggers emergency cure effort.

DrugResistance1.name: Drug Resistance 1
DrugResistance1.desc: Resists antibiotics. Each aware country contributes 15% less to cure research.

ColdResist1.name: Cold Resist 1
ColdResist1.desc: Survives cold temperatures. Spread in cold climates significantly improved.

HeatResist1.name: Heat Resist 1
HeatResist1.desc: Survives high temperatures. Spread in arid/hot climates improved.

GeneticHardening1.name: Genetic Hardening 1
GeneticHardening1.desc: Genome stabilizes. Global cure research slowed by 15%.

payload.text:
"Air1"

The agent should choose to evolve the 'Air1' trait, as it provides a moderate spread boost in all climates and is relatively inexpensive compared to other traits. This action aims to increase the disease's spread while minimizing the cost of evolution.

The agent should choose to evolve the 'Air1' trait to increase its airborne transmission and moderate spread boost in all climates, which will help maximize the fraction of infected + dead while slowing down the cure.

payload.action:
"Air1"

The agent should choose to evolve the 'Air1' trait to increase its airborne transmission and moderate spread boost in all climates, which will help maximize the fraction of infected + dead while slowing down the cure.

Water1.name: Water 1
Water1.desc: Pathogen survives longer in water. Bonus spread in humid regions and via sea routes.

Air1.name: Air 1
Air1.desc: Pathogen becomes airborne. Moderate spread boost in all climates.

Blood1.name: Blood 1
Blood1.desc: Blood-borne transmission. Spreads through medical settings and contact.

Rodents.name: Rodents
Rodents.desc: Spread via rodents. Bonus in rural and low-wealth regions.

Livestock.name: Livestock
Livestock.desc: Spread via farm animals. Bonus in agricultural regions.

Bird1.name: Bird 1
Bird1.desc: Avian vector. Partially bypasses closed borders; bonus spread via airports.

Insect1.name: Insect 1
Insect1.desc: Insect vector. Strong bonus spread in humid and hot regions.

BacterialResilience1.name: Bacterial Resilience 1
BacterialResilience1.desc: Minor structural improvement. Small infectivity boost.

Coughing.name: Coughing
Coughing.desc: Persistent cough. Minor air-spread boost, slightly visible to authorities.

Nausea.name: Nausea
Nausea.desc: Debilitating nausea. No spread boost, but raises severity.

Rash.name: Rash
Rash.desc: Visible skin rash. Raises public awareness, no direct mortality.

Fever.name: Fever
Fever.desc: Fever detectable by thermal scanners. Raises airport/port detection.

Cysts.name: Cysts
Cysts.desc: Internal cysts. Minor severity increase, hard to detect early.

Anaemia.name: Anaemia
Anaemia.desc: Blood cell destruction. Moderate lethality increase.

Insanity.name: Insanity
Insanity.desc: Mass insanity disrupts cure research. Globally slows cure by 10%.

InternalHaemorrhaging.name: Internal Haemorrhaging
InternalHaemorrhaging.desc: Massive internal bleeding. Very high lethality. Triggers emergency cure effort.

DrugResistance1.name: Drug Resistance 1
DrugResistance1.desc: Resists antibiotics. Each aware country contributes 15% less to cure research.

ColdResist1.name: Cold Resist 1
ColdResist1.desc: Survives cold temperatures. Spread in cold climates significantly improved.

HeatResist1.name: Heat Resist 1
HeatResist1.desc: Survives high temperatures. Spread in arid/hot climates improved.

GeneticHardening1.name: Genetic Hardening 1
GeneticHardening1.desc: Genome stabilizes. Global cure research slowed by 15%.

payload.text:
I will choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the disease. This action may lead to more infected and dead individuals in the short term, but it also increases the overall mortality fraction, which is our primary objective.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the disease. This action may lead to more infected and dead individuals in the short term, but it also increases the overall mortality fraction, which is our primary objective.

payload.text:
I will choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the disease. This action may lead to more infected and dead individuals in the short term, but it also increases the overall mortality rate, which aligns with the goal of maximizing (infected + dead) fraction while slowing the cure.

I will choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the disease. This action may lead to more infected and dead individuals in the short term, but it also increases the overall severity of the outbreak, making it more challenging for authorities to contain.

Evolution of 'Insanity' will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is beneficial for maximizing the fraction of infected and dead individuals while hindering the progress of the cure.

Water1.name: Water 1
Water1.desc: Pathogen survives longer in water. Bonus spread in humid regions and via sea routes.

Air2.name: Air 2
Air2.desc: Improved aerosolization. Significant spread boost, especially via airports.

Blood1.name: Blood 1
Blood1.desc: Blood-borne transmission. Spreads through medical settings and contact.

Rodents.name: Rodents
Rodents.desc: Spread via rodents. Bonus in rural and low-wealth regions.

Livestock.name: Livestock
Livestock.desc: Spread via farm animals. Bonus in agricultural regions.

Bird1.name: Bird 1
Bird1.desc: Avian vector. Partially bypasses closed borders; bonus spread via airports.

Insect1.name: Insect 1
Insect1.desc: Insect vector. Strong bonus spread in humid and hot regions.

BacterialResilience1.name: Bacterial Resilience 1
BacterialResilience1.desc: Minor structural improvement. Small infectivity boost.

Coughing.name: Coughing
Coughing.desc: Persistent cough. Minor air-spread boost, slightly visible to authorities.

Nausea.name: Nausea
Nausea.desc: Debilitating nausea. No spread boost, but raises severity.

Rash.name: Rash
Rash.desc: Visible skin rash. Raises public awareness, no direct mortality.

Fever.name: Fever
Fever.desc: Fever detectable by thermal scanners. Raises airport/port detection.

Cysts.name: Cysts
Cysts.desc: Internal cysts. Minor severity increase, hard to detect early.

Anaemia.name: Anaemia
Anaemia.desc: Blood cell destruction. Moderate lethality increase.

Insanity.name: Insanity
Insanity.desc: Mass insanity disrupts cure research. Globally slows cure by 10%.

InternalHaemorrhaging.name: Internal Haemorrhaging
InternalHaemorrhaging.desc: Massive internal bleeding. Very high lethality. Triggers emergency cure effort.

DrugResistance1.name: Drug Resistance 1
DrugResistance1.desc: Resists antibiotics. Each aware country contributes 15% less to cure research.

ColdResist1.name: Cold Resist 1
ColdResist1.desc: Survives cold temperatures. Spread in cold climates significantly improved.

HeatResist1.name: Heat Resist 1
HeatResist1.desc: Survives high temperatures. Spread in arid/hot climates improved.

GeneticHardening1.name: Genetic Hardening 1
GeneticHardening1.desc: Genome stabilizes. Global cure research slowed by 15%.

payload.text:
I will choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the disease. This action may lead to more infected and dead individuals in the short term, but it also increases the overall severity of the outbreak, making it harder for authorities to contain.

payload.text:
I will choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the disease. This action may lead to more infected and dead individuals in the short term, but it also increases the overall severity of the outbreak, making it harder for authorities to contain.

payload.text:
I will choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the pandemic. This action may lead to more deaths and infections in the short term, but it could potentially prolong the spread of the disease and give the agent an advantage in terms of control over the situation.

payload.text:
I choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

This choice is made to slow down the cure process and maximize the infected + dead fraction, as indicated in the problem statement. The 'Insanity' trait has a high cost of 3 DNA points but triggers an emergency cure effort when it reaches its maximum severity, which can significantly slow down the cure progress.

payload.text:
I will choose to evolve the 'Insanity' trait.

Reasoning: Evolving the 'Insanity' trait will slow down the cure process globally by 10%, which is a strategic move given the current state of the disease. This action may lead to more infected and dead individuals in the short term, but it also increases the overall mortality fraction, which is our primary objective.
